items.back: State runway on initial contact or as soon as practical. If not by 10 NM from runway transition waypoint, provide radar vectors to final.

items.front: Military single-pilot: min frequency changes below
Military single-pilot: avoid frequency changes during
items.back: When the aircraft is making a turn.

items.front: Military nonradar approach: tower freq change conditions
items.back: Ceiling >= 1,500 ft & vis >= 5 SM, or aircraft reports visual, or contact/visual approach cleared.
Add 10 to the first two digits of the flight level. FL 370 -> 37 + 10 = 47 miles.

items.front: Military turbojet descent: weather reporting trigger
items.back: Ceiling below 1,000 ft (USAF: 1,500 ft) or visibility less than 3 miles.

items.front: Military turbojet descent: termination without consent
items.back: Only for radar outage or emergency situation.

items.front: Approach info: when to include ceiling/vis
items.back: Ceiling < 1,000 ft or below highest circling minimum (whichever greater), or vis < 3 miles.
Advise when runway's ILS is not operational if another ILS on same frequency serves different runway.
Before/upon approach clearance, before en route descent, prior to departure (terminal), and after changes.
RwyCC may be issued in lieu of complete NOTAM; issue complete on pilot request workload permitting.
